evidence_summary: Type I interferons (the family IFN-omega belongs to) are well-documented inducers of IFN-gamma in NK/NKT/T cells, and IFN-omega specifically enhances cytolytic CD8+ T cell IFN-gamma responses. The relay through pDC is plausible since pDCs are the pri
citations: Type 1 Interferon Induction of Natural Killer Cell Gamma Interferon Production during LCMV Infection — https://journals.asm.org/doi/10.1128/mbio.00169-11; Human type-I interferon omega promotes cytolytic CD8+ T cell responses (bioRxiv 2025) — https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2025.02.13.638030v1; IFN-alpha treatment stimulates IFN-gamma in type I NKT cells — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5333814/

evidence_summary: IL-2 is well-established to induce IFN-gamma production in CD8+ T cells via STAT5/T-bet signaling, with multiple papers showing autocrine and paracrine IL-2 amplifies IFN-gamma and granzyme B output in CD8+ effector and memory T cells.
citations: IL-2 Produced by CD8+ Immune T cells Can Augment Their IFN-γ Production — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3578122/; CD4+ T Cell-Derived IL-2 Signals during Early Priming Advances Primary CD8+ T Cell Responses — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2770320/

evidence_summary: Type I interferons (including IFN-omega, signaling through the same IFNAR receptor as IFN-alpha/beta) upregulate IL-15 expression in vitro and in vivo, priming CD8 T and NK cells.
citations: Interferon-alpha/beta upregulate IL-15 expression in vitro and in vivo - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11030826/; IFN-alpha primes T- and NK-cells for IL-15-mediated signaling and cytotoxicity - https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0161589011006705; Soluble IL-15 Complexes Generated by Type I IFN Pathways - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4354909/

evidence_summary: IL-6 directly induces decorin mRNA in human endothelial cells in a dose-dependent manner (max at 10 ng/ml), although translation requires extracellular collagen signals. This is a well-documented direct induction of B by A, though not specifically in
citations: Interleukin (IL)-6 and IL-10 induce decorin mRNA in endothelial cells — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15016829/

evidence_summary: IL-36α (50 ng/ml) significantly increases endothelial tube formation in a VEGF-dependent manner; IL-36 family upregulates VEGFs and VEGFR2/3 in monocyte-rich inflammatory milieus (psoriasis).
citations: IL-36γ Is a Strong Inducer of IL-23 in Psoriatic Cells and Activates Angiogenesis — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5834930/; IL-36γ Augments Ocular Angiogenesis by Promoting the VEGF–VEGFR Axis — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10616713/; IL-1 Family Cytokine Regulation of Vascular Permeability and Angiogenesis — https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2019.01426/full

evidence_summary: TNF-alpha potently promotes Th9 cell differentiation and IL-9 production through TNFR2-dependent pathways. iTregs and Foxp3+ regulatory T cells are among the cell populations producing IL-9 downstream of TNF-alpha signaling.
citations: TNF-α enhances Th9 cell differentiation and antitumor immunity via TNFR2-dependent pathways — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6360681/; Transcription factor Foxo1 is essential for IL-9 induction in T helper cells — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5634439/

evidence_summary: Tumor-derived IL-35 promotes VEGF secretion and angiogenesis via STAT3 activation in myeloid cells (CD11b+Gr1+, neutrophils). Context-dependent: pro-angiogenic in tumors, anti-VEGF in rheumatoid arthritis.
citations: Tumor-derived IL-35 promotes tumor growth by enhancing myeloid cell accumulation and angiogenesis - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3578001/; IL-35 enhances angiogenic effects of small extracellular vesicles in breast cancer - https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35037402/; Mathematical modeling of IL-35 promoting tumor growth and angiogenesis - https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0110126

evidence_summary: Type-I IFNs (IFN-α/β) act as 'signal 3' on CD8 T cells; IFN-α subtypes induce significantly higher frequencies of IL-2-expressing CD8+ T cells (>2-fold increase) and synergize with IL-2 signaling for effector differentiation.
citations: Diverse Immunomodulatory Effects of Individual IFNα Subtypes on Virus-Specific CD8+ T Cell Responses — https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2019.02255/full; Regulation of Effector and Memory CD8 T Cell Differentiation by IL-2 — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6306427/

evidence_summary: IL-6 is a well-established inducer of VEGF expression via trans-signaling and STAT3 activation, both directly and synergistically with IL-1B and TNF.
citations: Interleukin 6 induces the expression of vascular endothelial growth factor — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/8557680/; IL-6 in Inflammation, Immunity, and Disease — https://cshperspectives.cshlp.org/content/6/10/a016295.full; IL-6 induces VEGF-C via Src-FAK-STAT3 — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4934912/

evidence_summary: VEGF enhances Th2 sensitization and inflammation in the lung, with a documented positive feedback loop with IL-13. VEGF induces IL-13-dependent asthma-like phenotype in transgenic mice.
citations: VEGF induces remodeling and enhances TH2-mediated sensitization - https://www.nature.com/articles/nm1105; VEGF controls lung Th2 inflammation via miR-1-Mpl-P-selectin axis - https://rupress.org/jem/article/210/10/1993/41405/VEGF-controls-lung-Th2-inflammation-via-the-miR-1; Studies of VEGF in asthma and COPD - https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22052929/

evidence_summary: CD30L/CD30 signaling is critical for maintenance and activation of IL-17A-producing γδ T cells in mucosa-associated tissues; CD30 stimulation directly induces IL-17 production in human cells (in vitro RA studies). Documented for γδ rather than CD8 sp
citations: CD30L/CD30 is critical for maintenance of IL-17A-producing γδ T cells — https://www.nature.com/articles/mi201318; CD30 Is Required for Activation of IL-17A-producing γδ T cells in Innate Immunity — https://journals.asm.org/doi/10.1128/iai.00887-13

evidence_summary: IL-36 (including IL-36alpha) activates Th17 cells and induces IL-17A expression, forming a positive feedback loop with IL-17A in psoriasis. Direct evidence in T cells via Th17 differentiation.
citations: Biology of IL-36 Signaling and Its Role in Systemic Inflammatory Diseases - https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2019.02532/full; Inter-Regulation of Th17 Cytokines and IL-36 Cytokines - https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022202X15351137; IL-17/IL-36 axis in skin inflammation - https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0222969

evidence_summary: IL-27 has been shown to augment TNF-alpha-induced IL-6 production in epithelial cells; the IL-6/IL-27 axis is well-documented bidirectional within the gp130 cytokine family, with both directions reported.
citations: Early IL-6 signalling promotes IL-27 dependent maturation of regulatory T cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5633202/; Regulation of inflammatory immune responses by IL-27 — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3332455/

evidence_summary: IL-29/IFN-λ1 up-regulates IL-6 production in PBMCs, macrophages, and synovial fibroblasts; IFN-λ directly responds in CD8 T cells. Direct induction in CD8 T cells specifically is not as well documented but plausible.
citations: IL-29 enhances Toll-like receptor-mediated IL-6 and IL-8 production — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3978693/; Modulation of the human cytokine response by IFN-λ1/IL-29 — https://www.nature.com/articles/6364348

evidence_summary: GM-CSF induces TL1A (Tnfsf15) expression in macrophages, demonstrated in intestinal inflammation models. ILC3 effects on macrophage TL1A were GM-CSF-dependent.
citations: GM-CSF Calibrates Macrophage Defense and Wound Healing Programs - https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S221112472030838X; TL1A/TNFSF15 directly induces proinflammatory cytokines from CD3+CD161+ T cells - https://www.mucosalimmunology.org/article/S1933-0219(22)01244-2/fulltext

evidence_summary: IL-9 acts on bronchial epithelial cells (BEAS-2B) in vitro to induce the T-cell chemoattractants IL-16 and RANTES; IL-16 can be induced by IL-4/IL-9/TNF/serotonin. Documented in epithelial cells, not NKT specifically.
citations: Interleukin (IL)-4/IL-9 and Exogenous IL-16 Induce IL-16 Production by BEAS-2B Cells — https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0008874900917453; Role of interleukin-16 in human diseases — https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2025.1524026/full

evidence_summary: IL-16 stimulation of CD14+ CD4+ monocytes/maturing macrophages induces IL-1b, IL-6, IL-15, and TNF-alpha production, with mRNA detected within 4 hours and protein by 24 hours. This is a direct, well-documented A→B effect.
citations: Interleukin-16 stimulates the expression and production of pro-inflammatory cytokines by human monocytes — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2326980/

evidence_summary: IFN-beta and IFN-omega are both type I interferons that bind the same IFNAR receptor and are typically co-induced by viral signals via IRF3/IRF7, but IFN-beta is not documented as a direct upstream inducer of IFN-omega in CD8 T cells.
citations: Shared and Unique Features of Human Interferon-Beta and Interferon-Alpha Subtypes — https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2020.605673/full; Type I Interferon Signaling Pathways — https://www.rndsystems.com/pathways/type-i-interferon-signaling-pathways

evidence_summary: Both are interferons in the same signaling family. IFN-beta has cell-type-specific and context-dependent effects: in monocytes it has been shown to modulate inflammatory responses, while in CD4+ T cells it can suppress IFN-gamma. Direct induction of
citations: Interferon-Beta Induces Distinct Gene Expression Response Patterns in Human Monocytes versus T cells (PLOS One) — https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0062366; Salmonella Induced IL-23 and IL-1β Allow for IL-12 Production by Monocytes... through Induction of IFN-γ — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2791865/

evidence_summary: TL1A synergizes with IL-12 (and IL-18) to enhance IFN-gamma production in T/NK/MAIT cells, but TL1A does not directly induce IL-12 expression — they are co-acting partners on shared targets.
citations: TL1A Synergizes with IL-12 and IL-18 to Enhance IFN-gamma Production in Human T Cells and NK Cells — https://journals.aai.org/jimmunol/article/172/11/7002/80995/; TCR and Inflammatory Signals Tune Human MAIT Cells — https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2211124719310988

evidence_summary: Documented direction is reverse: IL-17C induces VEGF (via STAT3/miR-23a-3p) in colorectal cancer and IL-17 family drives angiogenesis. No published evidence of VEGF inducing IL-17C in immune cells.
citations: miR-23a-3p is a Key Regulator of IL-17C-Induced Tumor Angiogenesis — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7348989/; IL-17 Promotes Angiogenic Factors IL-6, IL-8, and VEGF Production — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5098156/

evidence_summary: Documented direction is reverse: decorin induces VEGF and modulates VEGFR2; VEGF participates in decorin down-regulation (autocrine feedback) rather than induction. Not described in CD8 T cells.
citations: Ectopic decorin expression up-regulates VEGF expression in mouse cerebral endothelial cells — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18021292/; Decorin (Wikipedia) — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decorin

evidence_summary: Direction is reversed in literature: IL-36 signaling (via IL-36R/MyD88/NF-kB) drives Th9 differentiation and IL-9 production in CD4+ T cells, not the other way around.
citations: IL-36gamma signaling controls iTreg-Th9 balance - https://www.nature.com/articles/mi201721; IL-36 cytokines imprint a colitogenic phenotype on CD4+ T helper cells - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9038530/

evidence_summary: IL-35 and IL-27 are both heterodimeric IL-12-family cytokines sharing the Ebi3 subunit; both function as negative regulators with overlapping receptor usage (gp130/IL-12Rb2/WSX-1), but A→B direction (IL-35 inducing IL-27) is not explicitly documented
citations: Interleukin-35 on B cell and T cell induction and regulation — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5547520/; IL-12 family cytokines: immunological playmakers (Nature Immunology) — https://www.nature.com/articles/ni.2366

evidence_summary: The documented direction is the reverse: IL-9 induces VEGF secretion from mast cells (PLOS One 2012). No evidence VEGF induces IL-9 in monocytes; reverse direction is published.
citations: IL-9 Induces VEGF Secretion from Human Mast Cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3297631/

evidence_summary: The direction is reversed in the literature: IL-27 INHIBITS VEGF expression via STAT1 signaling and has documented anti-angiogenic properties. Both molecules are in the same axis (inflammation-angiogenesis) but A→B induction is not supported; the inv
citations: IL-27 inhibits epithelial-mesenchymal transition and angiogenic factor production in a STAT1-dominant pathway in human non-small cell lung cancer — https://jeccr.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/1756-9966-32-97

evidence_summary: Direction is reversed: IL-27 is a robust SUPPRESSOR of GM-CSF in T cells (STAT1-dependent), well documented in EAE and CNS inflammation. Co-regulated but A→B is anti-cascade.
citations: Suppression of GM-CSF expression in murine and human T cells by IL-27 - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3424384/; IL-27, but not IL-35, inhibits neuroinflammation through modulating GM-CSF expression - https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-017-16702-w

evidence_summary: IL-17A drives IL-36 agonist production (IL-36α/β/γ) in keratinocytes, and the IL-17A/IL-36 axis is well-established in psoriasis. IL-36Ra (the antagonist) is part of the same regulatory circuit and IL-38 (a related antagonist) is upregulated by anti-
citations: IL-17A-driven psoriasis is critically dependent on IL-36 signaling — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10754973/; The psoriasis-associated IL-17A induces and cooperates with IL-36 cytokines — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5688102/

evidence_summary: IL-27 is documented to suppress Th2 responses (including IL-13 production), so the documented direction is reverse (IL-27 → suppresses IL-13). IL-27 is known to induce IL-10 in MAIT cells, but IL-13 inducing IL-27 in MAIT was not found.
citations: Interleukin-27 in T Cell Immunity — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4346869/; Human MAIT cell response profiles biased toward IL-17 or IL-10 — https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.2414230122

evidence_summary: Direction is reversed: IL-27 is a well-documented SUPPRESSOR of Th9 differentiation and IL-9 production in naive CD4+ T cells, partially via STAT1/T-bet.
citations: IL-27 inhibits Th9 differentiation while inducing Tr1 cells - https://www.researchgate.net/figure/L-27-inhibits-Th9-differentiation-while-inducing-Tr1-cells-Real-time-PCR-and-flow_fig7_232813959; Updated advancement of bifunctional IL-27 in inflammatory autoimmune diseases - https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2024.1366377/full

evidence_summary: Documented direction is reverse: IL-27 inhibits IL-22 production by upregulating SOCS1 in CD4+ T cells. No evidence IL-22 induces IL-27 in MAIT cells.
citations: Interleukin-22 Influences the Th1/Th17 Axis — https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2021.618110/full; Identification of IL-27 as a novel regulator of MHC class I/II — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10561092/

evidence_summary: The well-documented direction is the reverse: IL-27 induces IL-21 in CD8+ T cells (and in Tfh/Tr1 differentiation). However, IL-21 enhancement of IL-27 in B cells has also been reported, making the relationship bidirectional but with the canonical ca
citations: IL-27 induction of IL-21 from human CD8+ T cells induces granzyme B in an autocrine manner — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7293547/; BATF3-dependent induction of IL-27 in B cells — https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.06.26.117010.full.pdf

evidence_summary: IL-12 and IL-27 are sister IL-12 family cytokines co-regulated by TLR signaling in DCs; both act on CD8 T cells (CD39 induction, effector differentiation) but IL-12 does not directly induce IL-27 expression.
citations: IL-12 and IL-27 Promote CD39 Expression on CD8+ T Cells — https://journals.aai.org/jimmunol/article/210/10/1598/263576/; Regulation of Antitumor Immune Responses by the IL-12 Family — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2946577/

evidence_summary: The documented direction is reverse: IL-36-alpha induces IL-6 (in fibroblasts and synovial cells, via MyD88/NF-kB). IL-6 inducing IL-36-alpha is not the documented cascade.
citations: IL-36a and IL-36g Induce Proinflammatory Mediators from Human Colonic Subepithelial Myofibroblasts — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4585048/; IL-36a Exerts Pro-Inflammatory Effects in the Lungs of Mice — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3447790/

evidence_summary: The documented direction is reverse: OX40L is a potent inducer of IL-9 (TH9 induction via TRAF6/NF-kB). IL-9 inducing OX40L is not the documented cascade direction.
citations: OX40 signaling favors the induction of TH9 cells and airway inflammation (Nature Immunology) — https://www.nature.com/articles/ni.2390; IL-33-primed human mast cells drive IL-9 production by CD4+ T cells in an OX40L-dependent manner — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC11479898/

evidence_summary: Documented effect is OPPOSITE direction: IL-27 suppresses GATA3 and IL-4/Th2 responses rather than inducing IL-4 in NKT cells. The cytokines are antagonistically linked.
citations: IL-27: Its Roles in the Induction and Inhibition of Inflammation — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2480558/; IL-27 Limits Type 2 Immunopathology Following Parainfluenza Virus Infection — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5305264/

evidence_summary: Documented direction is reverse: EGF induces VEGF (and NRP-1) in cancer cells. No published evidence VEGF induces EGF in NK cells.
citations: Induction of neuropilin-1 and VEGF by EGF in human gastric cancer cells — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2376351/

evidence_summary: IL-36Ra is the natural antagonist of IL-36 agonists, which themselves induce VEGF and angiogenesis. IL-36Ra INHIBITS VEGF expression by blocking IL-36 signaling — opposite direction of A→B induction. The molecules are coupled via the IL-36/VEGF axis.
citations: IL-36γ Augments Ocular Angiogenesis by Promoting the VEGF–VEGFR Axis — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10616713/; IL-36γ Is a Strong Inducer of IL-23 in Psoriatic Cells and Activates Angiogenesis — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5834930/

evidence_summary: TL1A/TNFSF15 drives IL-13 production primarily from ILC2s and Th2/Th9 cells (well-documented in muco-secretory fibrotic disorders and gut inflammation). The relay cell type in this case is CD14 Mono, where monocytes/macrophages are the primary SOURCE
citations: Targeting TL1A/DR3 Signaling Offers a Therapeutic Advantage to Neutralizing IL13/IL4Rα — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8299868/; The tale of TL1A in inflammation — https://www.nature.com/articles/mi201120

evidence_summary: GM-CSF promotes Th17 cell survival and IL-17 production in autoimmunity (via IL-6/IL-23-dependent pathway in DCs), but the canonical direction is Th17→GM-CSF; the relationship is bidirectional in autoimmune feedback loops.
citations: GM-CSF mediates autoimmunity by enhancing IL-6–dependent Th17 cell development — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2556786/; The encephalitogenicity of TH17 cells is dependent on IL-1- and IL-23-induced production of GM-CSF — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3116521/

evidence_summary: Documented direction is reverse: IL-4 induces APRIL expression in macrophages via p38MAPK-CREB/Stat6. No published evidence APRIL induces IL-4 in NKT cells.
citations: IL-4 stimulates mouse macrophages to express APRIL through p38MAPK and two different downstream molecules — https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1043466609001069; APRIL stimulates NF-κB-mediated HoxC4 induction for AID expression in mouse B cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3723325/

evidence_summary: Direction is reversed: GITRL/GITR co-stimulation drives Th9 differentiation and IL-9 production from CD4 T cells (key axis in anti-tumor immunity), not the other way around. IL-9 and GITRL co-occur in the Th9 axis.
citations: GITR drives TH9-mediated antitumor immunity - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4910720/; GITR subverts Foxp3+ Tregs to boost Th9 immunity - https://www.nature.com/articles/ncomms9266

evidence_summary: IL-36Ra is an antagonist that suppresses (not induces) IL-6 by inhibiting IL-36 signaling at IL-36R. The documented relationship is IL-36 agonists → IL-6 induction; IL-36Ra reduces IL-6. Thus IL-36Ra → IL-6 induction is the opposite of documented bio
citations: Protective effect of interleukin-36 receptor antagonist on liver injury — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7374990/; Interleukin(IL)-36α and IL-36γ Induce Proinflammatory Mediators — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4585048/

evidence_summary: Direction is reversed: cardiotrophin-1 induces VEGF expression in cardiac myocytes (via STAT3), well documented. No evidence of VEGF inducing CT-1.
citations: Cardiotrophin-1 induces VEGF expression in cardiac myocytes - https://www.pnas.org/doi/pdf/10.1073/pnas.92.4.1142; VEGF-A in Cardiomyocytes and Heart Diseases - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7432634/

evidence_summary: IL-12 is documented as ANTI-angiogenic (opposite direction to A→B), acting via IFN-γ/IP-10 to inhibit neovascularization and VEGF-dependent angiogenesis. Both molecules are coupled in the angiogenesis axis but the documented cascade is IL-12 inhibiti
citations: IL-12 Inhibition of Endothelial Cell Functions and Angiogenesis — https://journals.aai.org/jimmunol/article/166/6/3890/70412/; Angiostatin anti-angiogenesis requires IL-12 — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2630934/

evidence_summary: Documented direction is reverse: IL-15 signaling via JAK-STAT5 drives VEGF-A expression in NK cells. No published evidence VEGF induces IL-15.
citations: Transcription Factors Associated With IL-15 Cytokine Signaling During NK Cell Development — https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2021.610789/full; The Production of Pro-angiogenic VEGF-A Isoforms by Hypoxic Human NK Cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7477355/

evidence_summary: Decorin has been linked to IL-8 modulation in cancer/macrophage settings, with one study showing reduced decorin increases IL-8 in macrophages and another showing decorin-silenced oral cancer cells lose IL-8 expression. Effects are context-dependent
citations: A role for aberrantly expressed nuclear localized decorin in migration and invasion of dysplastic and malignant oral epithelial cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3198745/; Decorin Secreted by Human Umbilical Cord Blood-Derived Mesenchymal Stem Cells Induces Macrophage Polarization via CD44 — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6801980/

evidence_summary: APRIL and VEGF-A are both elevated in disease (e.g., extracellular vesicles in TNBC, gliomas) and are correlated prognostic biomarkers, but no mechanistic link showing APRIL directly induces VEGF in monocytes was found.
citations: A Retrospective Exploratory Analysis for Serum Extracellular Vesicles Reveals APRIL (TNFSF13), CXCL13, and VEGF-A as Prognostic Biomarkers — https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/24/21/15576

evidence_summary: GM-CSF is used to differentiate monocytes/DCs that can subsequently produce TSLP in response to LPS or other stimuli, and GM-CSF/TSLP/STAT3 signaling has been linked in cancer contexts. However direct GM-CSF→TSLP induction in immune cells (especially
citations: Thymic Stromal Lymphopoietin is Produced by Dendritic Cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3140600/

evidence_summary: APRIL and IL-6 are co-regulated in inflammation and both contribute to plasma cell survival, with positive correlations in arthritis models. No mechanistic evidence of IL-6 directly inducing APRIL in CD8 naive T cells was found.
citations: APRIL (TNFSF13) regulates collagen induced arthritis, IL-17 production and TH2 response — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2755626/

evidence_summary: Indirect link: IL-27 induces Th1 differentiation via STAT1/T-bet, and Th1 cells produce lymphotoxin (alongside IFN-gamma). No direct study of IL-27 inducing LT-alpha2-beta1 specifically in CD8 cells.
citations: Interleukin-27 in T Cell Immunity - https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4346869/; IL-27 elicits a cytotoxic CD8+ T cell program - https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-08510-w

evidence_summary: IL-6 promotes Th2 polarization (inducing IL-4 secretion and inhibiting Th1), and Th2 cells are the canonical IL-13 source. No direct mechanistic study of IL-6 inducing IL-13 transcription in naive CD4 T cells.
citations: Interleukin 6: at the interface of human health and disease - https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2023.1255533/full

evidence_summary: IL-16 is known to modulate surface receptor and cytokine release in macrophages/DCs, and IL-16 promotes inflammatory cell recruitment to similar tissues as IL-8, but a direct IL-16→IL-8 cascade in CD8 naive T cells was not found.
citations: Recombinant interleukin-16 selectively modulates surface receptor expression and cytokine release in macrophages and dendritic cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2326843/

evidence_summary: GM-CSF differentiates and primes myeloid cells; neutrophils are known to secrete APRIL in response to stimuli (TGF-beta, glycosaminoglycans), but a direct GM-CSF → APRIL induction (especially via NKT cells) is not directly documented. Indirect link v
citations: Neutrophil TLR4 and PKR are targets of glycosaminoglycans and effectors of APRIL secretion — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6002394/

evidence_summary: VEGF enhances TH2-mediated sensitization/inflammation in lung (Lee et al., Nature Medicine 2004) and VEGF inhibition decreases IL-4 production, supporting indirect VEGF→IL-4 enhancement. However, direct evidence in NKT cells specifically was not foun
citations: Vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) induces remodeling and enhances TH2-mediated sensitization and inflammation in the lung — https://www.nature.com/articles/nm1105

evidence_summary: C5a promotes IL-22 and IL-17 expression and provides costimulatory/survival signals to CD4+ T cells; IL-36α is produced by CD4+ T cells in inflammation. No direct evidence C5a induces IL-36α specifically, but pathways converge on CD4 T cell inflammat
citations: Complement component C5a Promotes Expression of IL-22 and IL-17 from Human T cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3154861/; Elevated IL-36α And CD4+IL-36α+T Cells in Graves' Disease — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6190875/

evidence_summary: IL-12 is listed among cytokines that can increase IL-9 production from CD4+ T cells under certain conditions, but the canonical Th9 differentiation pathway involves TGF-β + IL-4 (not IL-12, which classically drives Th1/IFN-γ).
citations: Th9 cells: differentiation and disease — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3982928/

evidence_summary: APRIL and IL-21 cooperate in B cell differentiation (APRIL+IL-21 induces IgA class switching and IL-10 in Bregs), but no direct evidence that APRIL induces IL-21 production in CD4 naive T cells was found. IL-21 is mainly induced by IL-6 and IL-27 in
citations: APRIL Induces a Novel Subset of IgA+ Regulatory B Cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6587076/

evidence_summary: IL-15 induces monocyte/macrophage activation and chemokine production (IL-8, MCP-1), and APRIL is produced by monocytes/macrophages. Both share myeloid expression but no direct mechanistic evidence found for IL-15 induction of APRIL in CD14 monocytes
citations: Interleukin-15 (IL-15) Induces IL-8 and Monocyte Chemotactic Protein 1 Production in Human Monocytes — https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0006497120579937

evidence_summary: No literature found linking APRIL (TNFSF13) to induction of Decorin in immune cells or any context. APRIL drives B cell/plasma cell biology and TACI/BCMA signaling; Decorin is an ECM proteoglycan regulated by TGF-beta, not APRIL.
Both IL-27 and IL-36α act on CD4+ T cells independently (IL-27 inhibits Th17/induces IL-10; IL-36α drives colitogenic Th cells) but no published evidence shows IL-27 induces IL-36α expression.
citations: Interleukin-27 in T Cell Immunity — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4346869/; IL-36 cytokines imprint a colitogenic phenotype on CD4+ T helper cells — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9038530/

evidence_summary: No published evidence that VEGF induces IL-20 in monocytes. VEGF induces MCP-1, IL-6, and IL-8 in vascular cells/monocytes, but IL-20 was not identified as a downstream target.
OSM is known to expand Treg and modulate Th17/Th2 balance, but no published evidence directly links OSM to IL-9 induction in Treg or other immune cells.

No direct evidence found that IFN-lambda1 (IL-29) induces IL-27 in MAIT or other immune cells. IFN-lambda1 induces ELR(-) CXC chemokines and inhibits Th2 responses, but IL-27 induction was not described.
APRIL is known to act on B cells and induce IL-10/PD-L1 in regulatory B cells, but no direct evidence found for APRIL induction of IL-20 in monocytes.
APRIL is known for B-cell biology (IgA class switching, Bregs) and modest T-cell costimulation, but no published literature links APRIL to IL-26 induction in CD8 T cells.

VEGF predominantly suppresses T cell function (via VEGFR2) and induces IL-6/IL-8/GRO-α in endothelium, not leukocytes. No evidence VEGF induces IL-22 in MAIT cells; IL-22 induction in MAIT is driven by IL-7/IL-12/IL-18/IL-23.
citations: VEGF directly suppresses activation of T cells from ascites — https://www.nature.com/articles/bjc2012468; VEGF MAIT search — https://immunenetwork.org/DOIx.php?id=10.4110%2Fin.2026.26.e8

evidence_summary: MAIT cells highly express IL17RE (receptor for IL-17C) and IL-17C is epithelium-derived (induced by IL-1beta/TNF/TLR2/TLR5), not by IL-9. No evidence IL-9 induces IL-17C in MAIT cells or elsewhere.
citations: MAIT cells and the microbiome (IL-17RE expression) - https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2023.1127588/full; IL-17C: Unique Epithelial Cytokine - https://www.jidonline.org/article/S0022-202X(18)30223-9/fulltext

evidence_summary: APRIL/TNFSF13 mainly acts on B cells (class switching, plasma cell survival, IgA+ Bregs) and was found to regulate IL-17/Th2 responses; no published evidence APRIL induces IL-22 in MAIT cells.
citations: APRIL (TNFSF13) regulates collagen-induced arthritis, IL-17 production and Th2 response — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19016524/

evidence_summary: Both APRIL and IFN-lambda1 are associated with plasmacytoid dendritic cells, but there is no published evidence that APRIL induces IFN-lambda1 in pDCs. IFN-lambda induction in pDCs is documented for TLR9 ligands (e.g., HHV-6B), not APRIL.
No literature found linking IL-9 to decorin expression in immune cells. Decorin is an ECM proteoglycan with TGF-beta scavenger function; no IL-9-related regulatory axis documented.

No published link found between APRIL and EPO induction in monocytes. APRIL is produced by monocytes (not the reverse), and EPO is hypoxia-regulated by HIF in kidney/liver; the two are studied independently.
IL-4 (not APRIL) induces IL-24 in macrophages and IL-4 also induces APRIL — they are downstream of a common signal but no direct APRIL→IL-24 induction is documented.
Tregs express LTα1β2 (membrane lymphotoxin) and use LTβR signaling for migration/tumor crosstalk, but no published evidence LTα2β1 induces IL-9 in Tregs.
citations: Regulatory T cells crosstalk with tumor cells and endothelium through lymphotoxin signaling — https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-024-54874-y

evidence_summary: VEGF modulates T cell IL-10/IFN-gamma balance and influences immunosuppression, but no published evidence found for VEGF induction of IFN-lambda1 (IL-29) in CD8 or any immune cell type.
APRIL is a TNF superfamily member like CD30L, but no published link between APRIL signaling and CD30L (TNFSF8) upregulation in naive CD4 T cells.

CD30L is expressed on activated monocytes (LPS-inducible) and IL-27 has effects on monocyte inflammatory programs, but no direct evidence found that IL-27 induces CD30L/TNFSF8 expression on monocytes.
Both APRIL and IL-36α are involved in inflammation but no published evidence that APRIL induces IL-36α expression in CD4 T cells.
IL-29/IFN-λ1 induction is canonically driven by viral/dsRNA stimuli through IRF/NF-κB pathways. No published evidence IL-9 induces IFN-λ1 in CD8 T cells.
citations: Interleukin 29 (Wikipedia) — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Interleukin_29

evidence_summary: FasL engagement induces DC maturation, IL-1β release, and IFN-γ, but no published evidence specifically linking FasL stimulation to IL-27 induction in conventional dendritic cells.

No clear literature identified linking IL-9 to APRIL induction in any cell type, let alone CD8 T cells. APRIL is primarily produced by myeloid cells in response to TLR agonists and inflammation, not via documented IL-9 signaling.
CD30L/CD153 is expressed on activated T/B/NK cells and is CD40-inducible; no published evidence IL-9 induces CD30L expression on HSPCs.
citations: CD30 Is a CD40-Inducible Molecule — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4621001/

evidence_summary: No literature directly linking APRIL induction of IL-35 in CD4 naive T cells. APRIL induces IL-10-producing IgA+ Bregs (which in turn induce Tregs/IL-35-producing populations) but the direct A→B axis was not found.
While CD30L is expressed on monocytes and can mediate various pleiotropic effects, no clear literature was found establishing CD30L→VEGF induction in CD14+ monocytes or any other immune cell type.

Decorin modulates macrophage polarization (M1/M2) and influences cytokine output in mesenchymal stem cells, but no direct evidence was found linking decorin to IL-27 induction, especially in MAIT cells.
No published evidence that C5a induces CD30L (TNFSF8) expression in ILCs. C5a is documented in neutrophil priming and IL-17/IL-23 regulation but not CD30L induction in ILCs.
APRIL is known to regulate B/T cell responses (collagen arthritis, IL-17, Th2 responses) but no documented evidence of APRIL inducing IL-27 production in immune cells, particularly MAIT cells.
APRIL induces IL-10/PD-L1 in IgA+ Bregs and modulates Th2 responses indirectly, but no published evidence that APRIL directly induces IL-13 in naive CD4 T cells.
IL-36Ra is a negative regulator that blocks IL-36R signaling. No published evidence IL-36Ra induces IL-27 in MAIT cells; the relationship would be expected to be indirect (release of inhibition).
citations: IL-36 cytokines in inflammatory and malignant diseases — https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00018-021-03909-4

evidence_summary: APRIL primarily acts on B cell survival, plasma cell maintenance, and IgA regulation. No documented evidence found linking APRIL to FasL induction in CD4 T cells or other immune contexts.

No published link between IL-19 (IL-10 family) and APRIL induction in any immune cell type, including MAIT cells. IL-19 signals through IL-20R complexes/STAT3; APRIL is induced by TLR9, IFN-alpha, and B cell activation independently.
No published evidence linking GITRL (TNFSF18) to APRIL induction in MAIT cells. GITRL regulates Treg/effector T cell responses; APRIL induction was not described downstream of GITR triggering.
No literature found connecting APRIL/TNFSF13 to EGF (epidermal growth factor) induction in T cells or immune cells. These molecules operate in distinct signaling families with no documented cascade.
Both VEGF and IL-19 participate in vascular inflammation but no direct evidence found for VEGF inducing IL-19 in NK cells or other immune cells.
CD40L is upregulated by IL-2, IL-12, IL-15 and downregulated by IL-4, IL-10; CD40L is expressed on activated T cells, not classically on cDCs. No published evidence IL-19 induces CD40L on cDCs.
citations: The role of CD40 and CD40L in Dendritic Cells — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2749083/

evidence_summary: VEGF and GM-CSF operate via distinct pathways with mostly opposing effects on dendritic cell function (VEGF suppresses, GM-CSF promotes maturation). No literature describes VEGF inducing GM-CSF in CD4 naive T cells.
Decorin has documented immunomodulatory effects on monocytes via MCP-1 induction and macrophage polarization, but no published evidence found linking decorin to IL-16 induction.

No literature found linking APRIL to IL-36Ra induction in NKT or any other immune cells. The two molecules operate in distinct pathways (APRIL: TACI/BCMA on B cells; IL-36Ra: IL-36R competitive antagonism in epithelia/B cells).
CD27/CD70 costimulation drives Th1 polarization and broad cytokine responses, but no published evidence that CD27L (CD70) induces IL-27 expression in monocytes.
No published evidence that IL-16 induces APRIL expression. IL-16 and APRIL are linked to B cell biology separately but no direct cascade in MAIT or other cells was found.
VEGF affects T-cell function (IL-10/IFN-gamma balance, suppression via VEGFR2), and NKT cells are known producers of IL-21, but no published direct link of VEGF inducing IL-21 in NKT cells was found.
GITRL is induced in keratinocytes by Th2 cytokines/TNF-alpha and on APCs by various stimuli, but no published evidence of VEGF inducing GITRL in NKT cells or any cell type.
No clear evidence of IL-16 inducing VEGF expression in immune cells, particularly MAIT cells. Multiple other interleukins (IL-6, IL-17, IL-1) are well-documented VEGF inducers, but IL-16 is not.
IL-9 is a known ILC2 product induced by IL-33/IL-25 alarmins, but no published evidence found that IL-19 induces IL-9 in ILCs.
CD70-CD27 costimulation broadens naive CD4 cytokine repertoire (Th1, Th2, IL-2, TNF-α) but no published evidence linking CD27L to IL-9 induction specifically.
CD70/CD27L is upregulated upon T/B cell activation, and IFN-gamma can induce CD70 on T cells. No documented evidence of APRIL inducing CD70/CD27L expression in NKT cells or other immune cells.
No published link between cardiotrophin-1 (IL-6 family/gp130) and APRIL induction. CT-1 induces IL-6, TNF-alpha, and VEGF via JAK2/STAT3, but no evidence for APRIL induction in MAIT or other cells.
IL-12 and IL-6 cooperate in T cell priming/Th1 development but no published evidence that IL-12 induces IL-6 production in NKT cells.

CD70/CD27L signals through CD27 leading to NF-kB/c-Jun activation, T cell costimulation and survival. No documented direct link to VEGF induction in MAIT cells or other immune cell types in the literature found.
APRIL modulates T cell proliferation and Th2/Th17 cytokine output in mouse models (in APRIL-/- mice, Th2 cytokines increase), but no published direct evidence of APRIL inducing IL-12 in CD4 T cells.
citations: APRIL (TNFSF13) regulates collagen induced arthritis, IL-17 production and TH2 response — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2755626/

evidence_summary: Both TL1A and APRIL are TNF superfamily members but no published link between TL1A signaling and APRIL induction in MAIT cells or elsewhere.
TL1A is induced by IL-1β/TNFα and synergizes with IL-12/IL-18 for NK IFN-γ/cytotoxicity. IL-36α drives Th1 polarization but no published evidence IL-36α induces TL1A in NK CD56bright cells.
citations: TL1A selectively enhances IL-12/IL-18-induced NK cell cytotoxicity — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20349123/; IL-36 signaling amplifies Th1 responses — https://ashpublications.org/blood/article/120/17/3478/30630/IL-36-signaling-amplifies-Th1-responses-by

evidence_summary: IL-9 and IL-15 share the gamma chain receptor but no evidence IL-9 induces IL-15 production. IL-15 (not IL-9) regulates NKT homeostasis, terminal maturation, and stage 3 differentiation.
Noggin is a BMP antagonist, not a classical immune cytokine. No evidence of a Noggin → IL-36-alpha axis in HSPCs in the immune literature.
Noggin is a BMP antagonist, not a classical immune cytokine. IL-27 modulates monocyte differentiation but no link to Noggin induction is documented.
Noggin is a BMP antagonist not classically considered an immune cytokine. Noggin/BMP signaling affects T cell IL-2 transcription, but APRIL has no documented role in inducing Noggin in CD8 T cells.
citations: Differential effects of inhibition of BMP signalling on T-cell activation — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22144105/

evidence_summary: Noggin is a BMP antagonist that opposes VEGF-driven vasculogenesis; it is not a classical immune cytokine. No published link between VEGF and Noggin induction in NK cells.
citations: In vitro and In vivo imaging of antivasculogenesis induced by Noggin in human venous endothelial cells — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19692649/

evidence_summary: Noggin is a BMP antagonist, not a classical immune cytokine. No evidence linking Noggin to CD40L induction on NK cells in immune literature. CD40L on NK is regulated by IL-12/IFN-γ.
citations: Regulation of CD40L expression on natural killer cells by interleukin-12 and interferon gamma — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11129327/

evidence_summary: Noggin is a BMP antagonist not classically considered an immune cytokine. IL-6 has many monocyte effects (MDSC induction, MCP-1 induction) but no published evidence IL-6 induces Noggin expression in CD14+ monocytes.
citations: IL-6 Induces Monocyte Chemotactic Protein-1 in PBMCs and U937 — https://ashpublications.org/blood/article/91/1/258/260347/Interleukin-6-Induces-Monocyte-Chemotactic-Protein
